name: Path of the Arroyo de las Cañas - circular from Palenciana (PR-A 8)
name_original: Sendero del Arroyo de las Cañas – circular desde Palenciana (PR-A 8)
region_intro: Hiking around Palenciana offers trails through a landscape characterized by the Genil River, extensive cereal fields, and olive groves in the south of Córdoba province. The terrain features riverside ecosystems with poplars and willows, alongside gentle hills like Cerro del Hacho. These natural elements combine to create diverse routes for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Palenciana The most popular hiking route is Route of the Norias of the Genil River, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route guides hikers through pine forests, olive groves, and riparian woodlands, featuring ancient Arab-origin waterwheels. Another top favourite among local hikers is Near the Genil River and Fuensanta Chapel — round trip from Badolatosa, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This trail explores the area close to the Genil River and the historic Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Fuensanta. Local hikers also love the Path of the Arroyo de las Cañas - circular from Palenciana (PR-A 8), a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Hiking around Palenciana is defined by riverside paths along the Genil River, routes through olive groves and cereal fields, and trails offering views from local hills. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Palenciana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Palenciana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Palenciana?
A: There are over 5 hiking routes documented around Palenciana, offering a variety of experiences through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on hikes in Palenciana?
A: Hiking in Palenciana offers diverse scenery, from extensive cereal fields and olive groves to the rich riverside ecosystems of the Genil River, featuring poplars, willows, and tamarisks. You'll also find trails with views of gentle hills like Cerro del Hacho.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Palenciana offers easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996398353/path-of-the-arroyo-de-las-canas-circular-from-palenciana-pr-a-8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Arroyo de las Cañas - circular from Palenciana (PR-A 8)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3 km circular trail that takes just over an hour to complete, perfect for a relaxed outing through agricultural landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer glimpses into the region's history and natural beauty. You can see ancient Arab-origin waterwheels along the Genil River, the ruins of the Gómez Arias castle on Cerro del Hacho, and the historic Hernán Ruiz II bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Fuensanta&lt;/a&gt;, an old Mozarabic cave church, is also a notable landmark near some routes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Palenciana?
A: Yes, Palenciana features several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996639915/route-of-the-norias-of-the-genil-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Norias of the Genil River&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.3 km circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996398353/path-of-the-arroyo-de-las-canas-circular-from-palenciana-pr-a-8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of the Arroyo de las Cañas - circular from Palenciana (PR-A 8)&lt;/a&gt; is an easier 4.3 km loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Palenciana?
A: The best time to hike in Palenciana is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during that season.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Palenciana dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on most rural trails in Andalusia, provided they are kept under control, especially near livestock, and owners clean up after them. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and consider the heat during warmer months.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Palenciana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil agricultural settings, the historical and ecological significance of the Genil River, and the opportunities to discover ancient hydraulic structures and historical sites embedded within its natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific natural features like waterfalls or unique water sources?
A: While large waterfalls are not a prominent feature, the Molinillo Path is associated with small waterfalls and pools. Additionally, about 2 km southeast of Palenciana, you can find the Fuente de los Ciélagos, a water source known for its mineral-medicinal, sulphurous waters.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Palenciana?
A: Specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, but as Palenciana is a village, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, especially near trailheads or local amenities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay in Palenciana after a hike?
A: Palenciana is a charming village, and while specific establishments are not listed, you can expect to find local cafes, restaurants, and possibly guesthouses or rural accommodations within the village to refresh and relax after your hike.

Q: What is the longest hiking route available in the area?
A: Among the listed routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander – Arroyo del Pontón loop from Corcoya&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, covering approximately 9.15 km and typically taking around 2 hours and 35 minutes to complete.
tips: The name of this town is due to the fact that its founders were from Palencia who arrived here after the reconquest as part of the colonization efforts. In their case, they dedicated themselves to the felling of holm oaks for the production of charcoal.
